source.notes: Optimus _ Jack Hedden  - 2026_06_09 11_29 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer (Optimus) took an evaluation call with Jack Hedden of ShipDLX, who is actively comparing data-enrichment vendors to run alongside his current provider. Jack drove almost the entire conversation—asking implementation-level questions about the Salesforce integration and proposing a data test—while Max answered accurately but reactively and never proactively pitched why Optimus should win. The call closed on a concrete but undated next step: Jack will send ~100 contacts/companies for a data test, and Max will check feasibility with engineering and follow up.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max answered each of Jack's questions directly and adapted his offer to what Jack asked for rather than reciting a script; acoustically he was calmer than his own 38-call baseline (arousal z -0.5) but spoke at a normal-to-brisk pace (pace z +1.3), consistent with an attentive if low-key delivery.

evidence.quote: keys within Salesforce and then copying the API into
I could just do some research if you sent over like companies or specific contacts

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Max described mechanics (trial unlocks, how push/pull works) but never made a quantified or concrete claim about why Optimus is better; the only differentiation voiced on the call came from the buyer, not the seller.

evidence.quote: There is a trial on Optimus that gives you five shipper unlocks. It would give you access to all of the contacts in those shippers.
you just push that contact over and it follows the under that company domain with the email and phone number if we have

relevance.explanation: Max never tied the offering to ShipDLX's specific freight network, lanes, or Jack's current data gaps; his responses stayed at generic platform mechanics and a generic request for a list.

evidence.quote: How many um shippers or contacts
can you just send me that contact list and then just a number you know I guess like 50 to 100

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked only logistical questions and did no real discovery of Jack's current pain, timeline, or decision criteria; he agreed to a concrete data test but left the follow-up undated rather than proposing a specific date.

evidence.quote: I could just talk to our engineers and see what we could gather

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Own the close: before ending an evaluation call, lock a specific follow-up date and state the quantified result that would make Optimus the winner (e.g., '% net-new phone numbers our shipper data typically surfaces'), so you set the evaluation bar instead of letting the buyer define it.

signals.signal: Actively shopping enrichment vendors and framed a clear evaluation path
signals.quote: we're right now in the process of looking at other data vendors to use as um, enrichment tools essentially um, in in tandem with our current data provider

signals.signal: Asked implementation-level questions, indicating serious intent to integrate
signals.quote: how do you set it up? I guess that's my question is like is it a mapping on your guys' end or do we have to custom build out stuff

signals.signal: Recognizes Optimus's differentiated data as a reason to engage
signals.quote: you guys offer like a whole another set of data that no one else does in terms of shipping and logistics related items

signals.signal: Committed to a buyer-owned next step and moved on it immediately
signals.quote: Well, I'll send that over right now.
